source_name: Content Security Policy Level 3 Editor Draft
explicit_claim_paraphrase: Multiple enforced CSP policies compose restrictively.
ACAO wildcard is not shareable with credentials mode include.
A serialized request origin with ACAC exactly true can be shareable for credentials mode include.
ACAC true is byte-case-sensitive for the shareability table.
Dynamic ACAO responses have cache interactions that require distinguishing Origin-varying responses.
STS headers received over insecure transport are ignored by user agents.
max-age=0 clears known HSTS host state including includeSubDomains.
Nonconforming STS header fields are ignored by user agents.
COEP require-corp blocks no-cors cross-origin resources without compatible CORP.
CORS mode can avoid COEP require-corp blockage when the resource supports CORS.
COOP same-origin is needed together with compatible COEP for cross-origin isolated features.
COOP unsafe-none is the default and opts out of process isolation.
Permissions-Policy allowlists determine whether features are allowed or denied in top-level and nested contexts.
An empty Permissions-Policy allowlist disables the feature.
Next.js nonce CSP requires dynamic rendering.
Next.js nonce CSP disables or conflicts with static optimization, ISR, default CDN caching, and PPR.
Helmet sets a default CSP header and the header usually needs app-specific configuration.
Helmet merges configured CSP directives into defaults unless defaults are disabled.
Helmet can emit CSP Report-Only through reportOnly=true.
Helmet does not set COEP by default.
Helmet performs little CSP validation and points users to CSP checkers.
Django SecurityMiddleware sets HSTS on HTTPS responses when SECURE_HSTS_SECONDS is nonzero.
Django may fail to add HSTS behind a load balancer or reverse proxy if secure-connection recognition is not configured.

source_name: Spring Security HTTP response headers documentation
explicit_claim_paraphrase: Spring Security does not add CSP by default because a reasonable default requires application context.

source_name: Spring Security HTTP response headers documentation
explicit_claim_paraphrase: Spring Security can configure CSP as enforced or report-only.
Rails recommends defining CSP and provides a DSL to configure the response header.
Rails can set CSP report-only during legacy-content migration.
Rails per-request nonce generation has caching and ETag tradeoffs.
Express cors origin=true reflects the request Origin.
Express cors credentials=true emits ACAC.
CORS is not API access control and does not stop the server from receiving requests.
CSP Evaluator reviews CSP as an XSS mitigation and common bypasses.
MDN HTTP Observatory checks security-relevant HTTP headers.
Chromium hstspreload is pinned as an HSTS preload eligibility library baseline.

source_name: Content Security Policy Level 3 Editor Draft
explicit_claim_paraphrase: Multiple enforced CSP policies restrict cumulatively, so a later layer can block a required script allowed by an earlier layer.

source_span: Helmet header-generation documentation and reportOnly examples
explicit_claim_paraphrase: Framework security-header APIs are one layer in a generated response surface that can be changed by application or deployment middleware.

source_span: Fetch Standard CORS cache guidance and dynamic ACAO behavior
explicit_claim_paraphrase: Dynamic credentialed CORS responses need Origin-sensitive cache treatment.
HSTS subdomain protection requires includeSubDomains when the intended scope includes subdomains.

source_span: pkg.go.dev exported eligibility helpers and hstspreload documentation
explicit_claim_paraphrase: An HSTS policy intended to be preload-ready must satisfy preload eligibility conditions such as sufficient max-age, includeSubDomains, and preload declaration.
